addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	{
2	  config,
3	  pkgs,
4	  lib,
5	  ...
6	}:
7	
8	{
9	  imports = [
10	    ./hypridle.nix
11	    ./hyprlock.nix
12	    ./hyprpaper.nix
13	    ./mako.nix
14	    ./waybar.nix
15	  ];
16	
17	  home.packages = with pkgs; [
18	    grim # Screenshot tool
19	    slurp # Region selector
20	    swappy # Snapshot editor
21	    wl-clipboard # Clipboard manager
22	    wf-recorder # Screen recorder
23	    ffmpeg # For GIF conversion
24	    jq # JSON processor
25	
26	    # Script to record GIF
27	    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "record-gif" ''
28	      #!/usr/bin/env bash
29	      # Define output
30	      DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)
31	      DIR="$HOME/Pictures/GIFs"
32	      MKV="$DIR/recording_$DATE.mkv"
33	      GIF="$DIR/recording_$DATE.gif"
34	
35	      mkdir -p "$DIR"
36	
37	      # Notify and Select Region
38	      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Select region to start recording..."
39	
40	      if ! REGION=$(slurp); then
41	          notify-send "GIF Recording" "Cancelled"
42	          exit 1
43	      fi
44	
45	      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Recording started! Press Super+Shift+S to stop."
46	
47	      # Record to MKV (more robust than MP4 for interruption)
48	      wf-recorder -g "$REGION" -f "$MKV"
49	
50	      # Convert to GIF after recording stops
51	      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Converting to GIF..."
52	
53	      # Generate palette for better quality
54	      palette="/tmp/palette.png"
55	      # higher quality scale
56	      filters="fps=15,scale=720:-1:flags=lanczos"
57	
58	      ffmpeg -v warning -i "$MKV" -vf "$filters,palettegen" -y "$palette"
59	      ffmpeg -v warning -i "$MKV" -i "$palette" -lavfi "$filters [x]; [x][1:v] paletteuse" -y "$GIF"
60	
61	      rm "$palette"
62	      rm "$MKV"
63	
64	      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Saved to $GIF"
65	      wl-copy "$GIF"
66	    '')
67	
68	    # Screenshot scripts (avoids quoting issues in Hyprland binds)
69	    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "screenshot-region" ''
70	      REGION=$(slurp) || exit 0
71	      grim -g "$REGION" - | wl-copy
72	    '')
73	    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "screenshot-region-edit" ''
74	      REGION=$(slurp) || exit 0
75	      grim -g "$REGION" - | swappy -f -
76	    '')
77	
78	    # Cheatsheet Script
79	    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "hypr-cheatsheet" ''
80	      #!/usr/bin/env bash
81	      # Parse keybindings from hyprctl using structured JSON
82	      hyprctl binds -j | jq -r '
83	        .[]
84	        | select(.has_description and .description != "")
85	        | (
86	            (if .modmask == 0 then ""
87	             else [
88	               (if (.modmask % 128) >= 64 then "Super" else empty end),
89	               (if (.modmask % 2) >= 1 then "Shift" else empty end),
90	               (if (.modmask % 8) >= 4 then "Ctrl" else empty end),
91	               (if (.modmask % 16) >= 8 then "Alt" else empty end)
92	             ] | join(" + ")
93	             end)
94	          ) as $mods
95	        | (if $mods == "" then .key else $mods + " + " + .key end)
96	          + " — " + .description
97	      ' | fuzzel --dmenu --width 80 --lines 20 --prompt "Keybindings: "
98	    '')
99	  ];
100	
101	  wayland.windowManager.hyprland = {
102	    enable = true;
103	    configType = "hyprlang";
104	    settings = {
105	      # --- Monitors ---
106	      # Auto-detect monitors with preferred resolution
107	      # Format: name, resolution, position, scale
108	      monitor = [
109	        "desc:Dell Inc. DELL U4025QW 37P0B84, highrr, auto, 1"
110	        ", preferred, auto, 1"
111	      ];
112	
113	      # --- Workspace Layouts ---
114	      workspace = [
115	        "1, layout:scrolling"
116	        "2, layout:master"
117	        "3, layout:master"
118	        "4, layout:master"
119	        "5, layout:dwindle"
120	      ];
121	
122	      # --- Input ---
123	      input = {
124	        kb_layout = "us";
125	        follow_mouse = 1;
126	        sensitivity = 0; # -1.0 - 1.0, 0 means no modification.
127	        natural_scroll = true;
128	      };
129	
130	      # --- General ---
131	      general = {
132	        gaps_in = 5;
133	        gaps_out = 10;
134	        border_size = 2;
135	        "col.active_border" = "rgb(cba6f7) rgb(94e2d5) 45deg"; # Catppuccin Mocha Mauve -> Teal
136	        "col.inactive_border" = "rgb(585b70)"; # Catppuccin Mocha Surface2
137	        layout = "master";
138	      };
139	
140	      # --- Master Layout ---
141	      master = {
142	        new_status = "slave";
143	        orientation = "left";
144	        mfact = 0.55;
145	      };
146	
147	      # --- Scrolling Layout ---
148	      scrolling = {
149	        column_width = "0.3";
150	        explicit_column_widths = "0.25, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.667, 1.0";
151	      };
152	
153	      # --- Decoration ---
154	      decoration = {
155	        rounding = 10;
156	        blur = {
157	          enabled = true;
158	          size = 3;
159	          passes = 1;
160	        };
161	        shadow = {
162	          enabled = true;
163	          range = 4;
164	          render_power = 3;
165	          color = "rgba(1e1e2eee)"; # Catppuccin Mocha Base
166	        };
167	      };
168	
169	      # --- Animations ---
170	      animations = {
171	        enabled = true;
172	        animation = [
173	          "workspaces, 1, 2, default, fade"
174	        ];
175	      };
176	
177	      # --- Cursor ---
178	      cursor = {
179	        no_hardware_cursors = true;
180	      };
181	
182	      # --- Layer Rules ---
183	      layerrule = [
184	        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace waybar"
185	        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace notifications"
186	      ];
187	
188	      # --- Window Rules ---
189	      # Hide Chrome's Spotify "now playing" floating popup (redundant with waybar mpris)
190	      windowrule = [
191	        "match:class ^(google-chrome)$, match:float yes, match:title .*•.*, workspace special:trash silent"
192	        "match:float yes, match:title .*is sharing your screen.*, workspace special:trash silent"
193	      ];
194	
195	      # --- Keybindings ---
196	      # NOTE: Keyboard is a Kinesis Advantage (split ergo). Super, Enter, Space,
197	      # Ctrl(R), PgUp, PgDn are all on the right thumb cluster. Avoid chords
198	      # combining any two of these keys (e.g. Super+Return, Super+Space).
199	      # Super+Backspace works (Backspace is on the left thumb cluster).
200	      #
201	      # Directional home row keys: J=left, K=down, L=up, ;=right
202	      # (Kinesis Advantage key wells make JKL; more natural than HJKL)
203	      #
204	      # Modifier scheme for JKL;:
205	      #   Super       = focus (spatial, works across all layouts)
206	      #   Super+Shift = move/swap window
207	      #   Super+Ctrl  = resize
208	      #   Super+Alt   = group/tab cycling
209	      "$mainMod" = "SUPER";
210	      bindd = [
211	        "$mainMod, Q, Open terminal, exec, kitty"
212	        "$mainMod, C, Close window, killactive,"
213	        "$mainMod, M, Lock screen, exec, hyprlock"
214	        "$mainMod, E, File manager, exec, thunar"
215	        "$mainMod, Y, Terminal file manager, exec, kitty -e yazi"
216	        "$mainMod, B, Open browser, exec, google-chrome-stable"
217	        "$mainMod, Z, Open Zen browser, exec, zen-beta"
218	        "$mainMod, V, Toggle floating, togglefloating,"
219	        "$mainMod, F, Toggle fullscreen, fullscreen, 1"
220	        "$mainMod SHIFT, F, True fullscreen, fullscreen, 0"
221	        "$mainMod, R, App launcher, exec, fuzzel"
222	        "$mainMod, slash, Keybindings cheatsheet, exec, hypr-cheatsheet"
223	        "$mainMod, P, Pseudotile, pseudo,"
224	
225	        # Focus
226	        "$mainMod, left, Focus left, movefocus, l"
227	        "$mainMod, right, Focus right, movefocus, r"
228	        "$mainMod, up, Focus up, movefocus, u"
229	        "$mainMod, down, Focus down, movefocus, d"
230	
231	        # Focus (home row — spatial movefocus works across all layouts)
232	        "$mainMod, J, Focus left, movefocus, l"
233	        "$mainMod, K, Focus down, movefocus, d"
234	        "$mainMod, L, Focus up, movefocus, u"
235	        "$mainMod, semicolon, Focus right, movefocus, r"
236	
237	        # Move window
238	        "$mainMod SHIFT, left, Move window left, movewindow, l"
239	        "$mainMod SHIFT, right, Move window right, movewindow, r"
240	        "$mainMod SHIFT, up, Move window up, movewindow, u"
241	        "$mainMod SHIFT, down, Move window down, movewindow, d"
242	
243	        # Move window (home row)
244	        "$mainMod SHIFT, J, Move window left, movewindow, l"
245	        "$mainMod SHIFT, K, Move window down, movewindow, d"
246	        "$mainMod SHIFT, L, Move window up, movewindow, u"
247	        "$mainMod SHIFT, semicolon, Move window right, movewindow, r"
248	
249	        # Master layout
250	        "$mainMod, BackSpace, Swap with master, layoutmsg, swapwithmaster master"
251	        "$mainMod SHIFT, BackSpace, Focus master, layoutmsg, focusmaster auto"
252	        "$mainMod, comma, Add master window, layoutmsg, addmaster"
253	        "$mainMod, period, Remove master window, layoutmsg, removemaster"
254	
255	        # Scroll layout (workspace 1)
256	        "$mainMod, equal, Fit visible columns, layoutmsg, fit visible"
257	        "$mainMod SHIFT, equal, Cycle column width, layoutmsg, colresize +conf"
258	
259	        # Workspaces
260	        "$mainMod, 1, Workspace 1, workspace, 1"
261	        "$mainMod, 2, Workspace 2, workspace, 2"
262	        "$mainMod, 3, Workspace 3, workspace, 3"
263	        "$mainMod, 4, Workspace 4, workspace, 4"
264	        "$mainMod, 5, Workspace 5, workspace, 5"
265	
266	        # Move to workspace
267	        "$mainMod SHIFT, 1, Move to workspace 1, movetoworkspace, 1"
268	        "$mainMod SHIFT, 2, Move to workspace 2, movetoworkspace, 2"
269	        "$mainMod SHIFT, 3, Move to workspace 3, movetoworkspace, 3"
270	        "$mainMod SHIFT, 4, Move to workspace 4, movetoworkspace, 4"
271	        "$mainMod SHIFT, 5, Move to workspace 5, movetoworkspace, 5"
272	
273	        # Screenshots
274	        ", Print, Screenshot region to clipboard, exec, screenshot-region"
275	        "$mainMod, Print, Screenshot region to editor, exec, screenshot-region-edit"
276	        "SHIFT, Print, Screenshot fullscreen, exec, grim - | wl-copy"
277	
278	        # Screenshot alternatives (no Print key)
279	        "$mainMod SHIFT, P, Screenshot region to clipboard, exec, screenshot-region"
280	        "$mainMod ALT, P, Screenshot region to editor, exec, screenshot-region-edit"
281	        "$mainMod CTRL, P, Screenshot fullscreen, exec, grim - | wl-copy"
282	
283	        # Screen recording
284	        "$mainMod SHIFT, G, Record GIF, exec, record-gif"
285	        "$mainMod SHIFT, S, Stop recording, exec, pkill --signal SIGINT wf-recorder"
286	
287	        # Workspace cycling
288	        "$mainMod, Tab, Next workspace, workspace, m+1"
289	        "$mainMod SHIFT, Tab, Previous workspace, workspace, m-1"
290	
291	        # Urgent
292	        "$mainMod, U, Focus urgent window, focusurgentorlast,"
293	
294	        # Groups (tabbed windows)
295	        "$mainMod, G, Toggle group, togglegroup,"
296	        "$mainMod ALT, J, Change active in group backward, changegroupactive, b"
297	        "$mainMod ALT, semicolon, Change active in group forward, changegroupactive, f"
298	
299	        # Notifications
300	        "$mainMod SHIFT, N, Restore notification, exec, makoctl restore"
301	        "$mainMod, N, Dismiss notification, exec, makoctl dismiss"
302	      ];
303	
304	      # --- Mouse Bindings ---
305	      bindm = [
306	        "ALT, mouse:272, movewindow"
307	        "ALT, mouse:273, resizewindow"
308	      ];
309	
310	      # --- Keyboard Resize (Super+Ctrl+JKL;) ---
311	      binde = [
312	        "$mainMod CTRL, J, resizeactive, -20 0"
313	        "$mainMod CTRL, K, resizeactive, 0 20"
314	        "$mainMod CTRL, L, resizeactive, 0 -20"
315	        "$mainMod CTRL, semicolon, resizeactive, 20 0"
316	      ];
317	
318	      debug = {
319	        disable_logs = true;
320	      };
321	    };
322	  };
323	
324	  systemd.user.targets.hyprland-session = {
325	    Unit = {
326	      Description = lib.mkForce "Hyprland session";
327	      BindsTo = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
328	      Wants = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
329	      After = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
330	    };
331	  };
332	}
333

input.command: git log --oneline main..HEAD && echo "---DIFF---" && git diff main..HEAD -- home/desktop.nix
input.description: Show branch commits and desktop.nix diff vs main

content.content:
33cff47 home/desktop: simplify hyprcursor theme copy in derivation
73f9e98 home/desktop: generate hyprcursor theme for Bibata cursor
fd45873 flake.lock: Update (#609)
7932826 flake.lock: Update (#608)
4f7eb98 flake.lock: Update (#607)
7e27de0 flake.lock: Update (#606)
d49b862 flake.lock: Update (#605)
fd98433 flake.lock: Update (#604)
a7e42f5 flake.lock: Update (#603)
16b799c flake.lock: Update (#602)
ae2865e flake.lock: Update (#600)
dc256ca flake.lock: Update (#599)
6401a9b Set Emacs as the default editor (EDITOR/VISUAL) (#598)
---DIFF---
diff --git a/home/desktop.nix b/home/desktop.nix
index e57658b..dc910e2 100644
--- a/home/desktop.nix
+++ b/home/desktop.nix
@@ -149,8 +149,38 @@
   home.pointerCursor = {
     gtk.enable = true;
     # x11.enable = true;
+    # Hyprland 0.55 uses hyprcursor by default, but bibata-cursors ships only an
+    # XCursor theme, so Hyprland can't resolve Bibata and falls back to a generic
+    # default cursor. Enabling hyprcursor here exports HYPRCURSOR_THEME/SIZE so
+    # Hyprland looks for a hyprcursor theme named "Bibata-Modern-Ice" (this
+    # home-manager version only sets the env vars; the theme itself is generated
+    # from the XCursor theme in the xdg.dataFile entry below).
+    hyprcursor.enable = true;
     package = pkgs.bibata-cursors;
     name = "Bibata-Modern-Ice";
     size = 24;
   };
+
+  # bibata-cursors is XCursor-only, so convert it to hyprcursor format for
+  # Hyprland 0.55 (which defaults to hyprcursor). hyprcursor-util --extract
+  # decompiles the XCursor theme (needs xcur2png) and --create compiles a
+  # hyprcursor theme. The manifest name is set to "Bibata-Modern-Ice" so
+  # Hyprland resolves HYPRCURSOR_THEME to this theme (hyprcursor matches on the
+  # manifest name or the directory stem). Installed under ~/.local/share/icons,
+  # which libhyprcursor searches; the XCursor theme keeps its own dir untouched.
+  xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor".source =
+    pkgs.runCommand "bibata-modern-ice-hyprcursor"
+      {
+        nativeBuildInputs = [
+          pkgs.hyprcursor
+          pkgs.xcur2png
+        ];
+      }
+      ''
+        hyprcursor-util --extract ${pkgs.bibata-cursors}/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice --output .
+        substituteInPlace extracted_Bibata-Modern-Ice/manifest.hl \
+          --replace-fail "name = Extracted Theme" "name = Bibata-Modern-Ice"
+        hyprcursor-util --create extracted_Bibata-Modern-Ice --output .
+        cp -r theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice "$out"
+      '';
 }

diff --git a/home/desktop.nix b/home/desktop.nix
index e57658b..dc910e2 100644
--- a/home/desktop.nix
+++ b/home/desktop.nix
@@ -149,8 +149,38 @@
   home.pointerCursor = {
     gtk.enable = true;
     # x11.enable = true;
+    # Hyprland 0.55 uses hyprcursor by default, but bibata-cursors ships only an
+    # XCursor theme, so Hyprland can't resolve Bibata and falls back to a generic
+    # default cursor. Enabling hyprcursor here exports HYPRCURSOR_THEME/SIZE so
+    # Hyprland looks for a hyprcursor theme named "Bibata-Modern-Ice" (this
+    # home-manager version only sets the env vars; the theme itself is generated
+    # from the XCursor theme in the xdg.dataFile entry below).
+    hyprcursor.enable = true;
     package = pkgs.bibata-cursors;
     name = "Bibata-Modern-Ice";
     size = 24;
   };
+
+  # bibata-cursors is XCursor-only, so convert it to hyprcursor format for
+  # Hyprland 0.55 (which defaults to hyprcursor). hyprcursor-util --extract
+  # decompiles the XCursor theme (needs xcur2png) and --create compiles a
+  # hyprcursor theme. The manifest name is set to "Bibata-Modern-Ice" so
+  # Hyprland resolves HYPRCURSOR_THEME to this theme (hyprcursor matches on the
+  # manifest name or the directory stem). Installed under ~/.local/share/icons,
+  # which libhyprcursor searches; the XCursor theme keeps its own dir untouched.
+  xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor".source =
+    pkgs.runCommand "bibata-modern-ice-hyprcursor"
+      {
+        nativeBuildInputs = [
+          pkgs.hyprcursor
+          pkgs.xcur2png
+        ];
+      }
+      ''
+        hyprcursor-util --extract ${pkgs.bibata-cursors}/share/icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice --output .
+        substituteInPlace extracted_Bibata-Modern-Ice/manifest.hl \
+          --replace-fail "name = Extracted Theme" "name = Bibata-Modern-Ice"
+        hyprcursor-util --create extracted_Bibata-Modern-Ice --output .
+        cp -r theme_Bibata-Modern-Ice "$out"
+      '';
 }

lines: -    # Hyprland 0.55 uses hyprcursor by default, but bibata-cursors ships only an
-    # XCursor theme, so Hyprland can't resolve Bibata and falls back to a generic
-    # default cursor. Enabling hyprcursor here exports HYPRCURSOR_THEME/SIZE so
-    # Hyprland looks for a hyprcursor theme named "Bibata-Modern-Ice" (this
-    # home-manager version only sets the env vars; the theme itself is generated
-    # from the XCursor theme in the xdg.dataFile entry below).
-  # bibata-cursors is XCursor-only, so convert it to hyprcursor format for
-  # decompiles the XCursor theme (needs xcur2png) and --create compiles a
-  # hyprcursor theme. The manifest name is set to "Bibata-Modern-Ice" so
-  # Hyprland resolves HYPRCURSOR_THEME to this theme (hyprcursor matches on the
-  # manifest name or the directory stem). Installed under ~/.local/share/icons,
-  # which libhyprcursor searches; the XCursor theme keeps its own dir untouched.

content.text:
Confirmed: the build failure is entirely in `python3.14-catppuccin-2.5.0` (matplotlib packaging bug in the pinned nixpkgs), a dependency of the `catppuccin-gtk` GTK theme — unrelated to my cursor change. It fails identically on main. My change is validated via `nix eval`, which proves both settings land in the generated config.

Let me review the final diff and check for a flake check / formatter.

input.command: git diff main -- home/hyprland.nix home/desktop.nix; echo "=== status vs HEAD ==="; git status --short; echo "=== formatter? ==="; ls .pre-commit-config.yaml treefmt.* .github/workflows 2>/dev/null; grep -rl "nixfmt\|treefmt\|formatter" flake.nix 2>/dev/null
input.description: Final diff vs main and check for formatter

content.content:
diff --git a/home/hyprland.nix b/home/hyprland.nix
index eb34117..076b188 100644
--- a/home/hyprland.nix
+++ b/home/hyprland.nix
@@ -175,10 +175,20 @@
       };
 
       # --- Cursor ---
+      # Hyprland 0.55 defaults to hyprcursor, but Bibata ships only an XCursor
+      # theme, so Hyprland finds no hyprcursor theme and falls back to a generic
+      # cursor. Disable hyprcursor and name the already-installed Bibata XCursor
+      # theme (via the env vars below) to restore the pre-upgrade cursor.
       cursor = {
         no_hardware_cursors = true;
+        enable_hyprcursor = false;
       };
 
+      env = [
+        "XCURSOR_THEME,Bibata-Modern-Ice"
+        "XCURSOR_SIZE,24"
+      ];
+
       # --- Layer Rules ---
       layerrule = [
         "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace waybar"

content.text: Formatting passes (0 changed — already nixfmt-clean). Diff is minimal and correct. Let me commit and push.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
home/hyprland: restore Bibata cursor via XCursor

Hyprland 0.55 defaults to hyprcursor, but bibata-cursors ships only an
XCursor theme, so Hyprland finds no hyprcursor theme and falls back to a
generic default cursor. Since XCURSOR_THEME was never exported in the
session, even the XCursor fallback had no theme name to load.

Disable hyprcursor and export XCURSOR_THEME/XCURSOR_SIZE via Hyprland's
env so it resolves the already-installed Bibata XCursor theme. Replaces
the earlier hyprcursor-generation approach with a minimal XCursor fix.
EOF
git log --oneline -1 && git push 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Commit and push the minimal cursor fix

input.command:
gh pr edit 611 --title "home/hyprland: restore Bibata cursor via XCursor (minimal, no hyprcursor theme gen)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

After a reboot activated a system upgrade, the mouse cursor changed to a generic default cursor.

**Root cause:** classic-laddie runs Hyprland 0.55, which defaults to **hyprcursor**. The configured cursor (`bibata-cursors` / `Bibata-Modern-Ice`) ships **only as an XCursor theme**, so Hyprland finds no hyprcursor theme and falls back to a generic default. On top of that, `XCURSOR_THEME` was never exported in the session, so even Hyprland's XCursor fallback had no theme name to load.

## Fix

Minimal, config-only — nothing is generated. The Bibata XCursor theme is already installed and on `XCURSOR_PATH`.

In `home/hyprland.nix`:
- `cursor.enable_hyprcursor = false` — tell Hyprland to use XCursor instead of hyprcursor.
- Export the theme name/size via Hyprland's `env`:
  - `XCURSOR_THEME,Bibata-Modern-Ice`
  - `XCURSOR_SIZE,24`

This replaces an earlier approach that ran `hyprcursor-util` to generate a hyprcursor theme in a derivation, per the maintainer's request to minimize config. `home/desktop.nix` is unchanged vs `main` (net-zero diff); `home.pointerCursor` stays as-is since it's still useful for GTK apps.

## Validation

- `nix eval` of the generated Hyprland settings confirms the values land:
  - `cursor: {"enable_hyprcursor": false, "no_hardware_cursors": true}`
  - `env: ["XCURSOR_THEME,Bibata-Modern-Ice", "XCURSOR_SIZE,24"]`
- Pre-commit hooks (`nixfmt`, `detect-private-keys`) pass.

Note: the full toplevel build currently fails on an unrelated pre-existing issue — `python3.14-catppuccin` (a dependency of `catppuccin-gtk`) fails to build due to a matplotlib packaging bug in the pinned nixpkgs. This is not affected by this change and fails identically on `main`.

## After rebuild

Re-login, or run `hyprctl setcursor Bibata-Modern-Ice 24`, for the cursor to take effect in the running session.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
Done. Summary of what I did:

## Changes

**`home/desktop.nix`** — reverted this PR's additions. Removed the `hyprcursor.enable = true;` line and the entire `xdg.dataFile."icons/Bibata-Modern-Ice-hyprcursor"` derivation (which ran `hyprcursor-util`). The file is now **byte-identical to `main`** (confirmed: `git diff main -- home/desktop.nix` is empty; grep for `hyprcursor`/`xdg.dataFile` finds nothing). `home.pointerCursor` is restored exactly as requested.

**`home/hyprland.nix`** — the minimal fix (10 added lines):
- `enable_hyprcursor = false;` added to the existing `cursor` block.
- A new `env` list with `"XCURSOR_THEME,Bibata-Modern-Ice"` and `"XCURSOR_SIZE,24"` (there was no pre-existing `env` key, so I created it — nothing to append to/clobber).
- A comment explaining the Hyprland-0.55-hyprcursor-default / xcursor-only-Bibata rationale.

## Validation
- **Settings land correctly** — verified via `nix eval` of the generated Hyprland settings:
  - `cursor: {"enable_hyprcursor": false, "no_hardware_cursors": true}`
  - `env: ["XCURSOR_THEME,Bibata-Modern-Ice", "XCURSOR_SIZE,24"]`
- **`nix fmt`** reports 0 files changed (already nixfmt-clean); pre-commit hooks (`nixfmt`, `detect-private-keys`) passed on commit.
- **Toplevel build** does not complete, but only because of a **pre-existing, unrelated** failure: `python3.14-catppuccin-2.5.0` (a dependency of the `catppuccin-gtk` theme) fails with a matplotlib packaging bug (`AttributeError: module 'matplotlib.style' has no attribute 'core'`) in the pinned nixpkgs. I confirmed this by building the theme package directly — it fails identically and my change doesn't touch it. I did **not** run switch/activate.

Committed as `07be49b` and pushed to `agent/20260711-1313-b7ae7d79`. PR #611 title and description updated to describe the minimal XCursor approach, including the re-login / `hyprctl setcursor Bibata-Modern-Ice 24` note.

One caveat worth flagging: I couldn't prove the settings via a *built* `hyprland.conf` because the toplevel won't build (the catppuccin issue). The `nix eval` on the same settings attrset is the authoritative proof that `enable_hyprcursor = false` and both `env` lines are what home-manager will render — the hyprlang generator serializes that attrset verbatim.
